title: Channel Budget Reallocation
summary: A decision workflow for moving budget between channels without reacting blindly to short-term noise.
whenToUseIt: Use this when one channel is underperforming, one channel is saturating, or you need to defend marketing spend with better allocation logic.

steps.title: Review current allocation
steps.description: List current spend by channel and compare it to output quality, not just raw lead volume.

steps.title: Check data quality and context
steps.description: Make sure the poor or strong result is real. Look for seasonality, campaign timing, tracking issues, or a short data window before changing budget.

steps.title: Rank channels by marginal return
steps.description: Estimate where the next dollar is most likely to generate a useful outcome and where additional spend is unlikely to help.
steps.tips: The best historical channel is not always where the next dollar should go.

steps.title: Reallocate in deliberate chunks
steps.description: Move budget in increments large enough to matter but small enough to reverse quickly if the assumption is wrong.

steps.title: Set the review checkpoint
steps.description: Decide when the reallocation will be reviewed and what metrics would confirm the move was correct.
steps.aiPrompt: Here is my channel spend and performance table: [paste data]. Recommend where to cut, where to hold, and where to increase spend, with reasoning tied to marginal return.

expectedOutcome: A budget change grounded in evidence, with a review window and explicit reasoning instead of reactive channel bias.
commonMistakes: Shifting budget based on one week's noise
Protecting legacy channels because they feel familiar
Moving spend without a review date or success threshold
Ignoring the quality of downstream pipeline or revenue
